title: Set Up and Use Claude Inside Excel for Finance Work
description: Install Claude in Excel and immediately start using it for data cleanup, formula help, model auditing, and report generation.

steps.title: Install the Claude Excel add-in
steps.content: Open Excel → Insert → Get Add-ins → Search "Claude by Anthropic" → Install. Open the sidebar with Ctrl+Option+C (Mac). Total setup: 2 minutes.

steps.title: Start with data cleanup
steps.content: This is where Claude in Excel is most immediately valuable. Feed it messy vendor names, inconsistent date formats, or duplicate entries. Claude catches everything. This alone saves hours every week.

steps.title: Use it for formula writing and debugging
steps.content: Describe what you need in plain English and get a traceable formula with an explanation. Stop Googling nested INDEX/MATCH — just ask.

steps.title: Run model audits
steps.content: Prompt: "Check this model for broken references, circular errors, and hard-coded numbers that should be formulas." Claude acts as a second pair of eyes that never gets tired. It finds things you miss.

steps.title: Build the Excel → PowerPoint pipeline
steps.content: Run a variance analysis in Excel, then ask Claude to pull the key findings into a board deck format. Same conversation, same session. This used to be a Sunday night task.

steps.title: Know where to be careful
steps.content: Complex multi-year forecasting still needs heavy human review. The outputs look polished but assumptions can have gaps. In finance, 99% correct is still wrong. Never blindly trust AI output on anything that goes external.
